fragility_points: A round-13 loss by Sindarov would reopen the market materially.
Hidden playoff or tiebreak nuances could reduce the apparent safety of the lead.
key_assumptions: A two-point lead with two rounds remaining leaves only a narrow failure set.
FIDE's reported standings and schedule are accurate and materially complete.
The Giri game is the main remaining swing event.
main_logical_chain: Use FIDE as the governing source of truth for current event state.
Observe that Sindarov maintained a two-point lead into the final two rounds.
Identify round 13 versus Giri as the only remaining major catalyst.
Apply a modest discount to the 95.05% market because the event is not yet formally settled.
main_thesis: Sindarov is overwhelmingly likely but not yet certain to win because the round-13 game against Giri remains the only clearly material live catalyst.
quote_anchors: With two rounds remaining, he moves one step closer to winning the Candidates
his final opportunity will come in the next round, where he faces a must-win situation
source_quality_view: Primary source quality is high because FIDE governs resolution, with medium evidence independence after independent Chess.com verification.
strongest_disconfirmers: The tournament is not officially finished and Sindarov still has to face Giri in a must-win spot for the chaser.
Official round reports are not yet the same as an official winner declaration.
strongest_supports: FIDE reports Sindarov preserved a two-point lead through round 12.
FIDE says Giri missed his chance in round 12 and now faces a must-win against Sindarov.
Chess.com says Sindarov is guaranteed at least a playoff.
timing_relevance: Immediate; most remaining probability movement is concentrated in the next round, especially Sindarov vs Giri.
unresolved_ambiguities: Exact official clinch math versus playoff math was not independently reconstructed from a formal standings table.
How much residual risk remains if Sindarov draws versus loses round 13.
what_would_change_view: An official FIDE update changing the standings interpretation, or a round-13 loss by Sindarov, would materially lower the estimate.
